source: Library of Congress, Prints & Photographs Division — Postcards Collection
license: No known restrictions on publication (public domain — pre-1928)
title: Russian block house, Sitka, Alaska
description: Historic gelatin silver postcard of the Russian blockhouse in Sitka, Alaska — one of the last surviving Russian-era military structures in North America. The octagonal log watchtower with pyramidal roof served as a defensive position along the stockade that separated the Russian settlement from the Tlingit village.
alt: Historic sepia postcard of the Russian log blockhouse watchtower in Sitka, Alaska with a man standing at the door for scale
verifiedBySession: 2026-04-12 — downloaded from tile.loc.gov and visually confirmed
